solutions.solution:
A) Condominiums would sell for $350,000 each.
B) There is a large demand for boat repair services.
C) A growing population results in the closure of shellfish.
D) There are already 1,500 moorings on the waterfront.
E) The shipyard may be sold for another commercial use.
Before the arrival of Joe, a new partner, sales output in Billy's Midas in Reverse Ltd., had been rising by 10% per year on average. Improvements by Joe included computerisation of technical procedures, reductions in the work force, but annual sales output has only risen by 2% per year. It appears that Joe's innovations have caused the reducing annual growth rate.

Which of the following, if true, would most seriously weaken this conclusion above?

A) The investment in new machinery entails a provision for depreciation and increased costs, which causes a reduction in profits.
B) Midas in Reverse Ltd. does not base increase in the selling price of its products with costs.
C) Joe's innovations were intended as long-term investment and not mainly for short-term profit.
D) General demand for the product manufactured by the company has declined.
E) Workers laid off by Midas in Reverse Ltd. have been hired by a competitor who is taking an increasing share of the market.
